summary: This is a brief announcement post where Cathy O'Neil shares that her TED talk has been published. The talk, titled 'The era of blind faith in big data must end,' addresses the dangers of uncritical acceptance of algorithmic decision-making. The post itself contains minimal content beyond the link and a personal note hoping readers will enjoy it.
key_points: The talk's thesis is captured in its title: blind faith in big data is problematic and must end
Represents O'Neil's broader campaign to bring algorithmic accountability to mainstream audiences
money_quotes: The era of blind faith in big data must end
key_insight: Society's uncritical trust in big data and algorithms needs to be challenged and replaced with skepticism and accountability.
